intro: “Pseudo-Chrysostom” labels works handed down under the name of John Chrysostom but not actually written by him. Chief among them is the Opus Imperfectum in Matthaeum (“the Unfinished Work on Matthew”), an anonymous fifth-century Latin commentary — probably by an author with Arian sympathies — that circulated under Chrysostom’s prestige and was quoted heavily in the medieval West, including Aquinas’s Catena Aurea. The prefix “Pseudo-” is a scholarly flag for uncertain authorship, not the name of a single person.
